title: External AI applications
subtitle: Use compatible work from other AI applications without copying it into BitFun.
checkingNonBlocking: Still checking for changes. You can continue using settings while this finishes.

actions.updated: External source settings updated. Review the current status below.

unavailable.title: Sources unavailable
unavailable.desktopOnly: External sources are available in the desktop app.
unavailable.remoteWorkspace: Remote workspace sources are not supported yet. No local configuration was loaded in their place.

errors.nonBlocking: The current external-source state could not be confirmed. The last known state remains visible; refresh and review the details before relying on it.

diagnostics.summary: {{count}} source entries need attention. Review the technical details before relying on affected content.

sources.title: Detected sources
sources.description: Sources remain in their original locations. Changes are picked up automatically while this page is open.
sources.globalOnly: Showing user-global sources. Open a workspace to include its project sources.
sources.empty: No compatible external sources were detected.

tools.title: Detected tools
tools.description: Tool code stays in its original location. Recognition never executes it.
tools.targetScope: This action applies to every tool exported by this module.

toolApprovals.title: Tools waiting for review
toolApprovals.description: Review the source and execution access before loading code from another application.
toolApprovals.warning: Tool code runs with your user permissions and inherited environment variables. It is not an operating-system sandbox, and descendant processes may outlive cancellation.

toolReason.approval_required: The module needs your approval before BitFun loads it.
toolReason.disabled: The target was disabled by user choice.
toolReason.active: The module loaded successfully and is exposed to the current execution domain.
toolReason.conflict: Another implementation uses the same tool name.
toolReason.unsupported: The module uses syntax or behavior this runtime does not support.
toolReason.runtime_unavailable: The required JavaScript runtime is unavailable.
toolReason.load_failed: The tool process could not load this module.

toolNextStep.approval_required: Review access, then enable the target or keep it disabled.
toolNextStep.disabled: Enable the target after reviewing its source and access.
toolNextStep.active: No action is required; disable the module to stop exposing its tools.
toolNextStep.conflict: Choose a provider below, or disable this target.
toolNextStep.unsupported: Convert the module to the supported standalone JavaScript subset, then refresh.
toolNextStep.runtime_unavailable: Restore the required runtime, then refresh.
toolNextStep.load_failed: Fix the module and refresh, or disable this target.

toolConflicts.title: Tool conflicts need your choice
toolConflicts.description: An existing local or MCP implementation remains in place when available; otherwise the name stays unavailable. Your choice is remembered until a candidate changes.
toolConflicts.pending: Choose the implementation BitFun should expose for matching candidates in this execution domain.

conflicts.title: Needs your choice
conflicts.description: Same-name extensions are not activated silently. Your choice is remembered until a participant changes version.
conflicts.pending: Choose one available source to make this command available.
